issue_title: Possible mem leak on the workloads with huge results in tasks
issue_body:
I have a celeryflower instance that monitors a couple of celeryd workers. I use redis as backend results storage. So when there are a lot of tasks that returns huge results (up to limit of 512mb) - celeryflower begins to eat memory until all tasks finishes or i restart flower.

There are no problem when results are small (bool).

I solved that problem with memory plugin of supervisord. But there is no way to store complete history.

As i see, flower stores own copy of results in locmem memory or it is a memleak.How can i make flower not to handle tasks results? Such option in config will be usefull.

I have just pushed to develop a reorganization of the UnitRegistry
1.- It has moved to its own file
2.- It has been splitted into different registries, each providing different capabilities (base, non-multiplicative, systems, contexts). UnitRegistry (which is exported) provides all of them
3.- `load_definitions` is cleaner, as it provides an API to register new `@` directives and its parsers

It is a little bit slower (probably because there are more function calls). However I think it is worth it because:
1.- The code is cleaner.
2.- The code is easier to understand, fix and improve due to the separation of concerns.
3.- In principle, somebody could extend the registry easily.
4.- In principle, somebody could use a simple registry if it does not require all the functionality. 

Let me know what you think.

There are two more things I would like to do:
1.- Improving Error reporting See #374 
2.- See if there is room for performance improvement
3.- Remove the need to call _after_init using metaclasses.

issue_title: Paralogous genes (without introns) stealing mapping in multi-threaded splice mode
issue_body:
We've been looking at the recent mapping results of a MinION cDNA run, and discovered a few genes that were curiously absent in minimap2-generated files. These were genes that had high expression (according to STAR) in Illumina reads from the same cell line.

I dug into the details of one of these genes ([Eno1](http://asia.ensembl.org/Mus_musculus/Gene/Compara_Paralog?db=core;g=ENSMUSG00000063524;r=4:150236721-150248879;t=ENSMUST00000080926)), and discovered that it had an intron-less paralog (with >99% identity and coverage) in the mus musculus genome. Mapping a minion read separately to both regions produced positive matches in both cases, but only one match was returned when the regions were combined as a multi-fasta file. This issue remains when reducing down to a minimal test case containing just the two genes of interest, but disappears when I remove the threaded mode from minimap2.

While I don't expect minimap2 to prioritise the spliced mapping over the non-spliced mapping, it'd be nice if it included the spliced mapping as at least a secondary mapping.

issue_title: Wrong Python used with runipy command

I'm having trouble running a notebook and it seems even if I specify the full path to the `runipy` function with (`~/anaconda/bin/runipy`) and prepend my `$PYTHONPATH` variable, it uses a different Python! [Here](https://gist.github.com/olgabot/874b6bae493eadc247c8184de6e0a5f4) a gist with the whole output, which is excerpted below.

```

I ran into some problems running `checkpy` because the following lines failed:

https://github.com/Jelleas/CheckPy/blob/67f36f11de74e9ab143317252f314d31dbb5fdf4/checkpy/caches.py#L47-L49

It specifically failed when passing a list to a function that is wrapped by this cache. 

A `tuple` is not guaranteed to be hashable, it fails when the `tuple` contains an unhashable type:

```python
([]) in {}  # TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'
```

Solution: use `str()` to create hashable versions of any object (with `__str___`) 

Perhaps I'll create a PR sometime :-)

issue_title: Add support for Python context management (with statement)
issue_body:
I think it would be neat to incorporate the ability to use the 'with' statement to manage connections to other devices. This would involve adding the __exit__ method to automatically close out any open connections (or maybe some other action is appropriate). 

This alleviates the need for direct connection management (having to remember to close connections), and would be especially useful for connecting to multiple devices within the same script. It can also come in handy in error handling so that we don't need to provide extraneous "close connection" code in the event of exceptions within the context, or wrap everything in massive try/except blocks.

For example:
```

 [let's talk about "€" later ...](#about-alternate-engraving)
### note: 
Maybe I am wrong in the way I watch the topic following : didn't dig your code enough yet.
Whatever, it sounds weird, and I am curious about the boolean's usage :
```

but     0xbb: ('+', False),   is representation_false in the vkKeyScan view
   -->  0xbb: ('=', False),   true

```
Though, it seems difficult to hold both ```0xbb:('=',False)```  and  ```0xbb:('+',True)```, in the same dictionary on the same key... , what it should definitely be in the vkKeyScan view, _if I don't have a completely wrong view of that topic_. Please forget and forgive the confusion if it is the case...
Would it mean that you would also exclude:
```
                '''&é"'(-è_çà)=''' and $ friends ?
              ( '''1234567890°+''',    £ flavour )
```
### also:
in: _keyboard_event.py :
```
    canonical_names = { ...,
                     'num add': '+',
                     'num plus': '+',
                     'num minus': '+',       # oops
                     'num sub': '-' , 
```

also, ```0xa2: ('left ctrl', False)``` ... are very ambiguous to me : the ctrl keys shifted do **not** produce anything in fact, their only _real context of life_ is the MOD_CONTROL + MOD_SHIFT flag of a vkKeyScan ! 
I guess that your mapping is including these cases as to compact data. 

Other implementation use this k:v on a different purpose: watch for the ```alt-key-pressed``` state of a key, in the case of UI menus handling with a &MenuItem shortcut mapping.

There is an implementation of these crossed mappings between vk and ascii 
representation in the "PythonWin" [source code on github](https://github.com/ksmyth/pywin32/blob/master/Pythonwin/pywin/scintilla/keycodes.py),[and a shorter version too](http://www.math.uiuc.edu/~gfrancis/illimath/windows/aszgard_mini/pylibs/pythonwin/pywin/scintilla/keycodes.py) with fewer mappings.

Yet their mapping is incomplete, as it has a contextual purpose.
Yours aims to be wider, so nice !

Their logic is to parse vk as``` "ascii_not_shifted +-" (+-shifted) ```to be sure to end with no ambiguity (function _parse_key_name(name)_).
Ex: outputs of test2() in keycodes.py:
```
    Alt+/  ->  191,19  ->  Shift+Alt+:           '/' is detected as 'shift'+':'
    \      ->   56,15  ->  Ctrl+Alt+_          it couldn't match an event as 'alt-gr'+'_' ...
```

Also it separates the users logic from the implementation logic : 
a silly example:
I want to set up a hk with the ``` '/' ``` symbol for my purpose, not the ``` ':' ``` symbol.
I think (hope) most people type``` 'shift+:' ```without the intent to press this specific combination, they do because "this is the way it is to get ``` '/' ``` ", from the _reptilian cognitive layer_, almost like walking.
Though, ```'ctrl+/'``` would be meaningful, ex. as an application hotkey, where the / holds the aim, however we do access to it. It is ```'shift+:'```, you know it, could you hold that for my higher-level-of-abstraction ... ? 
And this way, I am sure that both symbols will be available, as they **should not 
be mutually exclusive**, every char will be safe and glad to serve our most exquisite desires !

### About alternate engraving
the "alternate-engraving" key and WM_SYSKEYDOWN : 
```
in _winkeyboard.py,
         line~~300, ... alt_gr_scan_code = 541         # ???
         line~~121, WM_SYSKEYDOWN = 0x104 # Used for ALT key
         watch the results I get from a view on the events broadcasting :
         it is true for ( 'ctrl' , 'alt' , 'e' ), not for ( 'alt-gr' , 'e' ), on my winxp sp3
```
I don't see that as an _inconsistency_, just the complexity to be able to input more symbols than the number of keys we have on our keyboards. As the `alt` key served the UI menus fisrt, it is a way to have a kind of alternate-alt, that shares only a part of it's business with it's left sister, not as the `ctrl` and `shift` keys do, and usable with one finger only !!!
The more I used it, the more I loved that key. I was doing maths outside of latex : Autohothey was a big help to map _contextually_ all my keys to utf-8 Greek and math symbols on ``` alt-gr/ctrl+alt ``` ! 
Then giving the users two different methods of input, one for the left-handlers, one for the others ( 'alt-gr' | 'ctrl'+'alt' ) over-adds to the complexity.

By the way, your unicode implementation is a really consistent piece of code to study, as it is still a pain for me to have a large view on it, a really, really nice good help. 
Thanks a lot for the lesson .
```
VK_CONTROL   0x11
VK_MENU      0x12  :  alt     menu because it first gave access to menus,

and be a good boy : mind the "traffic light", saving xml may hang for freeze !
```
# a quick filter script for the Large xml file :
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
file_in  = "messages3.xml"                  # same dir as script_file
file_out = "filtered_"+file_in
tree = ET.parse(file_in)                    # ASCII or use ET.XMLParser(enc...)
root = tree.getroot()
filter =  [ 'WM_SYSKEYDOWN' ,
            'WM_KEYDOWN'    ,
            'WM_CHAR'       ,
            'WM_KEYUP'      ,
            'WM_SYSKEYUP'   
            ]
for message in root.findall('message'):
    name = message.find('name').text
    if name not in filter:
        root.remove(message)
tree.write('filtered_messages.xml') # Result to an XML File

```

issue_title: Crash from bad error handling in "py::object Hasher<T>::CallWithArgs"
issue_body:
Hi !

There is a bug in the pyfasthash's way of handling error and throwing exceptions in `src/Hash.h`.
When an exception is set, a value other than NULL is returned leading to 2 bugs.
Example src/Hash.h (line 264 / 265)

```
  ::PyErr_SetString(::PyExc_TypeError, "wrong type of argument");
  return py::object(py::handle<>(Py_None));
```

The following code doesn't respect the CPython ABI saying that when an exception is raised, NULL must be returned.
The first consequence is putting the interpreter in an instable state leading to the exception being randomly raised elsewhere in the code
The second problem of this code is returning `None` without incrementing its ref-counter leading `None` to be sub-counted. If `hash(None)` is called enough time it will lead to CPython abort by trying to deallocate `None`.

Here is a POC:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python

import sys
import pyhash

print "Number of refs on None: {}".format(sys.getrefcount(None))
h = pyhash.fnv1_64()
for i in range(10000):
   h(None) #exception should be raised here
   try:
        if i % 100 == 0:
            #but is delayed to here
            print "Number of refs on None: {}".format(sys.getrefcount(None))
   except:
        print "[CATCH] Number of refs on None: {}".format(sys.getrefcount(None))

```

output is:

```
# python pyhash-deallocating-none-crash.py 
Number of refs on None: 2101
[CATCH] Number of refs on None: 2099
[CATCH] Number of refs on None: 1999
......
[CATCH] Number of refs on None: 199
[CATCH] Number of refs on None: 99
Fatal Python error: deallocating None
Aborted (core dumped)
```

We can observe the 2 bugs:
1) The exception is delayed to the str.format call ('h(None)' is not in the try - catch)
2) The ref-count of `None` decrease dangerously lead to CPython abort

Is there any interest in dropping support for old Python versions? When they reach EOL that means they're no longer receiving security updates.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1324225/34263159-38b766bc-e677-11e7-918b-54bc889cfff0.png)

setup.py has the same versions:

        'Programming Language :: Python :: 2.7',

  - "3.2"

---

Here's the pip installs for colorama from PyPI for the last month (via `pypinfo --percent --pip --markdown colorama pyversion`)

| python_version | percent | download_count |

Collate_chars' [sort](https://github.com/jsvine/pdfplumber/blob/master/pdfplumber/utils.py#L43) assumes that colinear words have the same exact doctop. I hacked around this with words by mucking with precision, but the variations here are much bigger--1 or 2 pixels. 

I'm hacking around this with an optional snap_to_y_grid arg, and something like: 

    if snap_to_y_grid:

issue_title: Proposal to treat each run through a scenario outline as a distinct scenario.
issue_body:
Currently when you iterate through a scenario outline like:

```
Scenario Outline: Winslow pages requiring user to be signed out
        Given I go to "<foo>"
       Then there should be "<bar>"

        Examples:
            | foo                  |  bar | 
            | example.com | Hi |
            | google.com   | how are you |
```

The hooks ie `@hook('before', 'scenario')` only fire at the beginning/end of the entire Scenario Outline.

In my mind it seems that each 'run' through a scenario outline should be treated as a distinct scenario.  Behavior that should be triggered before or after a scenario (as defined by the hooks) should be triggered for each outline example row.

The gherkin syntax isn't 100% clear on this https://github.com/cucumber/cucumber/wiki/Scenario-Outlines but the example at the top of the page that starts with "Copying and pasting scenarios to use different values quickly becomes tedious and repetitive:" leads some credibility to my assumptions.

Let me know if you need more details.

According to the 5.0 standard, this:
```
[VOLUME]
FILE = 'data/interim/volume.tif'
CRS = 'epsg:26949'

Z.MIN = -0.3048
Z.MAX = 1.524

```
should produce (indented for readability)
```
{ 'VOLUME': { 
        'FILE': 'data/interim/volume.tif',
        'CRS': 'epsg:26949',
        'Z': { 
            'MIN': -0.3048,

issue_title: Time init string parsing is broken
issue_body:
Some time ago I added a feature to parse string versions of time values like this:

    value = Time("01:02:03.04")

But apparently an old bug reared its ugly head.  It's broken, but a trivial fix.

This is inspired by [this StackOverflow question](https://stackoverflow.com/q/45734562/1399279). The problem is that even with `ns.LOCALE` some non-ASCII letters are not sorted as you might expect, but running it through `unicode.normalize` does the trick.

It is not clear if this should be the default behavior or an add-on.  It is also not clear if it should default to `'NFD'` or if it can be more flexible.

issue_title: generate_keypair Not Persisting Objects in SoftHSMv2
issue_body:
Hey there!

Thanks for the great work creating this library. I really like the Pythonic interface to otherwise verbose PKCS11 bindings.

I'm using SoftHSMv2 as a POC to take this library for a spin, and I'm running into some issues when it comes to persisting generated keypairs.

Here's some sample code to show you what I'm doing:

```python
lib = pkcs11.lib(os.environ['PKCS11_MODULE'])
token = lib.get_token(token_label='DEMO')
with token.open(user_pin='1234') as session:
    pub, priv = session.generate_keypair(
        pkcs11.KeyType.RSA, key_length=2048, label="DEMOKEY")

    #print(pkcs11.util.rsa.encode_rsa_public_key(pub))

with token.open(user_pin='1234') as session:
    keys = session.get_objects({pkcs11.Attribute.LABEL: 'DEMOKEY'})
    for key in keys:
        print(key)
```

In this example, I'm generating a new RSA keypair (which is presumably stored on the HSM), then I'm opening a new session and attempting to search for those keys and print any indication that they're there. Unfortunately, the `get_objects` iterator is empty.

It's worth noting that:
* The commented out RSA encoding works fine
* Even if I move the `get_objects` call into the same session as the `generate_keypair` call, it still returns an empty iterator.
* SoftHSMv2 persistence should be fine, since things seem to work ok when using a different pkcs11 library (specifically Pykcs11).

Any help would be greatly appreciated. If there's any other information you need from me to help track this down, please don't hesitate to let me know!

issue_title: Split filtering for standard and extended IDs
issue_body:
Right now there is no clear distinction between 11- and 29-bit messages for filtering. Seems like most interfaces have support for having different filters but right now they apply the same filter settings for both or have some other non-standard way of specifying which one to apply the filtering on.

Maybe it should be something like `[{"can_id_ext": 0x123456, "can_mask_ext": 0xFFFFFF}, {"can_id_std": 0x123, "can_mask_std": 0xFFF}]`.

If can_id and can_mask is specified, it could be automatically translated to both std and ext variants in can.interface.Bus constructor to be fairly backwards compatible.

issue_title: server only serves chunks in forward direction
issue_body:
This has been introduced in 04e7dbe9, as a fix for peer discovery, when misconfigured servers returned a mixture of testnet and mainnet servers to the client.

The client now requests a checkpoint (default checkpoint is the genesis block) when it initiates a connection, and it closes the connection if the returned header fails to pass the checkpoint. Therefore this fix is not needed anymore. 

Clients may need to request chunks in reverse order if there is a hard fork and they are on the shortest branch. Commit 04e7dbe9 prevents them from fetching the other branch.

issue_title: LegalMoveGenerator can run generate_legal_moves() twice when creating a list
issue_body:
Python-chess is great!

But I have found that

`list(board.legal_moves)`

or

`[*board.legal_moves]`

to get a list is unnecessarily slow because generate_legal_moves() is called twice. (First from `__len__` of the LegalMoveGenerator, and then from `__iter__`.)

`[x for x in legal_moves]`

on the other hand is much faster because this double calling does not happen.

If took me quite a bit of profiling with SnakeViz and stack traces to figure this out, so maybe LegalMoveGenerator should get a to_list() method that returns a list in the fastest way possible.

issue_title: no-duplication of data when building the kd-tree
issue_body:
Hei,

I am not even sure my request makes sense in a kd-tree world, but let's try ;)

pykdtree is sucessfully used in [pyresample](https://github.com/pytroll/pyresample/tree/master/pyresample) for searches of neighbours in the geo-spatial / cartesian domain. The indexes and distances are used e.g. to resample satellite information to regular grids.

however, pykdtree (as scipy kdtree, I assume) allows for duplicating the data, so that a satellite x/y/z observation can be simultaneously the neighbour of several areadef x/y/z. This is even when returning only the k=1 (nearest) neighbour for the 'nn' resampling. As a visible outcome, a user of pyresample.kd_tree.resample_nn() might experience smeared areas at the outskirt of a well defined satellites swath (see example below, the issue is best seen in the last two frames (50.0x)).

![example_nn](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/9883832/19077925/a1c3f7fa-8a4e-11e6-9db7-93de6979847e.gif)

So the question to pykdtree: is there any way (existing, or to be developed) for ensuring that a kd-tree will not contain duplicates of the source data. In our case, force that each satellite x/y/z will appear once at most, and that the occasion it appears is when it is actually closest to one of the "target" locations.

If there is no way from inside pykdtree, this will have to be implemented as a post-processing step (in pyresample...).

Cheers,
T.

issue_title: Parameterize the wormhole generator used by `xfer_util`
issue_body:
Presently, xfer_util imports the wormhole module and calls `create` on it.  After #154 is resolved, there will be a second implementation of the magic-wormhole Python interface.  It would be nice if there were some way to get xfer_util to use this second implementation instead of the primary (real) implementation.

This will be helpful for all of the reasons described on #154.  It is not *strictly* necessary since the wormhole module could always be monkey-patched.  However, explicitly passed parameters are clearly superior.

issue_title: Do not create zipfile when sending much data
issue_body: Sometimes people have less free space on disk than the size of the zipped data they want to send. In such cases it would be reasonable to stream data and calculate checksums on the fly rather than to create a zipfile first.

issue_title: Complex matrices failing to convert datatype
issue_body:
Hi, it seems that complex matrices may be broken with the current master. Here is an example:

`test.pyx`:

```
from eigency.core cimport *

cdef extern from "test.h":
     cdef MatrixXcd _test_matrix "test_matrix"(Map[MatrixXcd] &)

def test_matrix(np.ndarray array):
    return ndarray_copy(_test_matrix(Map[MatrixXcd](array)))
```

`test.h`:

```
#pragma once

#include <eigen3/Eigen/Dense>

Eigen::MatrixXcd test_matrix(const Eigen::MatrixXcd&);
```

`test.cpp`:

```
#include "test.h"

Eigen::MatrixXcd test_matrix(const Eigen::MatrixXcd& result) {
    return result;
}
```

Then in python:

```
import test_matrix
print test_matrix.test_matrix(np.zeros((3, 3)) + 0.4)
```

The output is not as expected (`0.4 + 0.0j` for all entries):

```
[[  4.00000000e-001 +4.00000000e-001j   4.00000000e-001 +4.00000000e-001j
    6.93540132e-310 +6.93540132e-310j]
 [  4.00000000e-001 +4.00000000e-001j   4.00000000e-001 +6.95335581e-309j
    0.00000000e+000 +6.93540132e-310j]
 [  4.00000000e-001 +4.00000000e-001j   6.93540132e-310 +0.00000000e+000j
    6.93540132e-310 +0.00000000e+000j]]
```

However, if I do something like the following:

```
m = np.zeros((3, 3)) + 0.4
print test_matrix.test_matrix(m.astype(np.complex128))
```

I get the correct output:

```
[[ 0.4+0.j  0.4+0.j  0.4+0.j]
 [ 0.4+0.j  0.4+0.j  0.4+0.j]
 [ 0.4+0.j  0.4+0.j  0.4+0.j]]
```

While this example shows mixing real with complex types, it also happens between different complex types, like `complex64` mixed with `complex128`.

Is this expected behavior?

Really enjoying the `dataclass` decorator and can't wait for it to be standard in Python 3.7. I've been playing around with it in 3.6 and came across a use-case that I can't seem to make work intuitively. Basically, `Point2D` is a point with a units field, and a default value to simplify things. `Blob` is a subclass of `Point2D` which is simply a point with a diameter. It needs to be a subclass so that it can inherit the helper methods on `Point2D` and so type-checking allows it to be used anywhere `Point2D` can be.

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum


class Unit(Enum):
    Millimeters = 'mm'
    Inches = 'in'


@dataclass
class Point2D:
    x: float
    y: float
    units: Unit = Unit.Inches

    def distance_to(self, point: 'Point2D') -> float:
        """ Calculate distance to another point """


@dataclass
class Blob(Point2D):
    diameter: float
```

This seems to run into two main issues. First, `Blob` can't add a required `diameter` field as it would cause a non-default argument to follow a default argument in the generated `__init__`. This could be worked around with a custom `__init__`, but that would still leave us with the second problem: the order of the fields in `repr` and `astuple`. Ideally we'd want those to be: `x`, `y`, `diameter`, `units` as that makes more sense than `x`, `y`, `units`, `diameter`.

What if class inheritance where the child class defines a superset of the parent's fields was a special case which re-defined the order of the fields? Would turn the above `Blob` definition into:

```python
@dataclass
class Blob(Point2D):
    x: float
    y: float
    diameter: float
    units: Unit = Unit.Inches
```

I suppose this is a bit 'dangerous' as it's magic and someone could inadvertently redefine the order of the fields while subclassing when they simply intended to give all fields a default, but listed them in a slightly different order. As such, perhaps it could be enabled by a new keyword arg to `dataclass`, such as `allow_reorder`.

Implementation could be pretty straightforward, before [line 802](https://github.com/ericvsmith/dataclasses/blob/master/dataclasses.py#L802) add something like:

```python
if set([f.name for f in cls_fields]).issuperset(set(fields.keys())):
    fields.clear()
```

Actually, while typing this all up I realized that the implementation was simple enough that I just went ahead and did it in my own fork, 183ccfea97feab12a657bd1971db816a7ae0998d. Only played with it a little but so far so good, it's not a very complicated change.

issue_title: Security/Integrity of using revert/rollback trick in front-facing views?
issue_body:
Thank you for the awesome app, and please forgive this "non-bug" post on your issue tracker:
I have a front-facing view (based on `reversion.admin.VersionAdmin._reversion_revisionform_view`) that lets users see a previous version of an object (and all its relations) by adding a `?rev=` GET parameter to the URL (my code is below).  It works great, but being relatively new to database management I have a few questions:

1.  When my view is executed, debug-toolbar reports about 10ms between the first `UPDATE` event and the final `ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT` event.  I know it's very unlikely with low-traffic websites, but if a second user were to request the page in those 10 ms, am I correct in thinking that they would get the rolled-back version without having explicitly asked for it?
2. It seems safe enough for an admin view, but from the viewpoint of database integrity, is it risky to have a public-facing view that lets users "abuse" the revert/rollback transaction mechanism like this?  Is there a race condition or other scenario that could leave the database in an inconsistent state?
3.  There are no POST requests accepted at this url/view, but since this page would not require login, could a malicious user take advantage of this view to screw up the database?

```python
class myModelDetailView(DetailView):
    queryset = MyModel.objects.all()

    def version_view(self, request, rev, *args, **kwargs):
       # step back "rev" number of revisions and render response
        versions = Version.objects.get_for_object(self.get_object())
        version = versions[min(versions.count() - 1, rev)]
        try:
            with transaction.atomic(using=version.db):
                # Revert the revision.
                version.revision.revert(delete=True)
                # Get the object and render the response
                self.object = self.get_object()
                context = self.get_context_data(object=self.object)
                response = self.render_to_response(context)
                response.render()  # eager rendering of response before db rollback
                raise _RollBackRevisionView(response)
        except _RollBackRevisionView as ex:
            return ex.response

    def get(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        try:
            # look for rev parameter in get string
            rev = int(self.request.GET.get('rev', False))
        except Exception:
            rev = 0
        if rev > 0:
            return self.version_view(request, rev, *args, **kwargs)
        return super().get(request, *args, **kwargs)
```

issue_title: swagger.json does not support custom URL converters
issue_body:
Similar issue to #233.

Minimal working example:
```python
from flask import Flask
from werkzeug.routing import BaseConverter
from flask_restplus import Api, Resource


class RegexConverter(BaseConverter):
    def __init__(self, url_map, *items):
        super(RegexConverter, self).__init__(url_map)
        self.regex = items[0]


app = Flask(__name__)
app.url_map.converters['regex'] = RegexConverter
api = Api(app, version='1.0', title='RegexExample')


@api.route('/<regex("[0-9]*"):uid>/')
# @api.route('/<uid>/')
class Example(Resource):
    def get(self, uid):
        return {'success': uid}


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)
```

* With `@api.route('/<uid>/')` everything is obviously working:

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2772505/23256381/7786129e-f9bf-11e6-9f98-e51efaa6748e.png)


* With `@api.route('/<regex("[0-9]*"):uid>/')` it all explodes:
```
  File "/home/user/.local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/flask_restplus/swagger.py", line 82, in extract_path_params
    raise ValueError('Unsupported type converter: {}, {}'.format(descriptor, name))
ValueError: Unsupported type converter: regex("[0-9]*"), uid
```

issue_title: Channel property access through context manager is probably an abuse of context managers
issue_body:
#334 added context managers for accessing channel properties
```python
# using a context manager.
with session['2'] as c2:
    c2.voltage_level = 5
    c2.output_enabled = True
```

Context managers are generally applied when you want to have code execute at the end of a block, especially if its symmetrical (open / close).  One less common use I admittedly make for context managers is to make my code structure look like my XML structure.

This use of a context manager though is more equivalent of VB's [with block](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/wc500chb(v=vs.90).aspx) and provides no value over assigning to a variable.
- A variable needs to be created anyways
- with-statements do not create a new scope and the variable created from one will be available outside of the with-statement.

yet it does imply something more might be going on (like reverting values at the end) when in actuality there isn't.

I may have missed but I couldn't find any way to configure APIGateway, and particularly the schemas.

Since schemas are usually very close to models, it would be nice to be able to associate a schema to a route inside app.py, rather than packaging and doing it in sam.json

This is the only step i need to truly have a one command deploy of my chalice app.

Thoughts?

issue_title: S3 Pre-Signed URL ClientMethod and HttpMethod difference?
issue_body:
Hello,

I knew that the S3 [generate_presigned_url](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/s3.html#S3.Client.generate_presigned_url) method in boto3 is providing few parameters can help us to define the S3 generating pre-signed URL.

It is very simple to generate a pre-signed URL like the following example:
```python
s3.generate_presigned_url(                                                                                                                                                                                                          
            ClientMethod='get_object',                                                                                                                                                                                                        
            ExpiresIn=3600,                                                                                                                                                                                                    
            Params={                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
                'Bucket': 'MyBucket',                                                                                                                                                                                                             
                'Key': 'object-key'                                                                                                                                                                                                                    
                }                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
            ),
            HttpMethod='GET'                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
```

In the example, we can specify the `HttpMethod` as `GET` or not to specify. However, I just wondering that what is the purpose to use the `HttpMethod` in the parameters? As it mentioned in the public document:

> HttpMethod (string) -- The http method to use on the generated url. By default, the http method is whatever is used in the method's model.

Does that mean we can use the **POST** method to do the **put_object** action? Or what is the scenario we will specify this argument for some specific purpose?

Since I tried to search this topic in the issue but seems no one is having the similar issue, I think it should describe more detail in the document. So if it is the duplicate, please help to refer the issue. Thanks.

The used code:
```python
import boto3
import multiprocessing as mp

def read_table(table_name, pe, seg=None, total_seg=None, limit=None):
    dynamodb = boto3.client('dynamodb')
    scan_args = {
        'TableName': table_name,
        'ProjectionExpression': pe
    }
    if seg and total_seg:
        scan_args['TotalSegments'] = total_seg
        scan_args['Segment'] = seg
    if limit:
        scan_args['Limit'] = limit

    items = []
    total_scanned_count = 0
    while True:
        response = dynamodb.scan(**scan_args)
        items += response['Items']
        total_scanned_count += response['ScannedCount']
        if limit:
            scan_args['Limit'] = limit - total_scanned_count
        
        if 'LastEvaluatedKey' not in response or limit == total_scanned_count:
            break

        scan_args['ExclusiveStartKey'] = response['LastEvaluatedKey']
                   
    print("Total for seg {}: {}".format(seg, len(items)))

    return items


def worker(seg=None, total_seg=None):
    print("Running on segment {}".format(seg))
    items = read_table('table-name', 'id', seg=seg, total_seg=total_seg)
    
    return items


def work(worker, args=(), pool_size=mp.cpu_count()):
    pool = mp.Pool(processes=pool_size)

    result = []
    for i in range(pool_size):
        callback = lambda x: result.append(x)
        error_callback = lambda x: print(x)
        kwds = {'seg': i, 'total_seg': pool_size}
        pool.apply_async(worker, args=args, kwds=kwds, callback=callback,
                         error_callback=error_callback)

    pool.close()
    pool.join()

    return result
```

issue_title: Incorrectly formatted canonical_query_string leads to incorrect signature calculation.
issue_body:
## Description:
Incorrectly formatted canonical_query_string leads to incorrect signature calculation.

## Example:
Origin **query_string** from request:
`fields=title&per_page=10&search=%22New%20Visualization%22&search_fields=title&type=visualization`

Formatted **query_string** that is included in [canonical_request](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/develop/botocore/auth.py#L298):
`fields=title&per_page=10&search=%22New+Visualization%22&search_fields=title&type=visualization`

As we can see from above example `%20` part was changed to `+`.

Therefore we have mismatch in signature calculation of canonical_request.

**Expected**:
```
AWS4-HMAC-SHA256
20180107T152408Z
20180107/eu-west-1/es/aws4_request
cef1f4d819179f631cdfe09b7a224133d0d61396a8c80504375e6a02fa15218f
```

**Actual**:
```
AWS4-HMAC-SHA256
20180107T152408Z
20180107/eu-west-1/es/aws4_request
a973700a5f53e24acccb5795657411f9a2fa293b63896a585c5a4622b625b86d
```

#### I've tested this issue and after I have changed canonical_query_string to origin I had correct signature calculation.


## Details (don't think this need but I'll follow your guides):
OS: macOS Sierra 10.12.6
Python interpreter: CPython 3.6.2
Botocore version: 1.4.93

issue_title: Dynamodb local uses HTTPS instead of HTTP
issue_body:
In our endpoints.json we have:

```
            "local": {
              "hostname": "localhost:8000",
              "protocols": [
                "http"
              ],
              "credentialScope": {
                "region": "us-east-1"
              }
            }

```

However, creating a client with this region will use HTTPS:

```
>>> import botocore.session
>>> botocore.session.get_session().create_client('dynamodb', 'local').list_tables()
...
botocore.vendored.requests.exceptions.SSLError: [SSL: UNKNOWN_PROTOCOL] unknown protocol (_ssl.c:581)
```

My best guess is that we're not honoring the `protocol` key from endpoints.json when creating clients.

DyNet basically aborts the program when some fatal errors occurred (e.g. OOM).
However, this behavior is sometimes not desirable because abort() deprives users of opportunities to deal with these problems themselves.
Would you like to switch all abort() terminations to throwing an exception if possible?

If the library would like to keep the current behavior, it may be possible to add an Boolean parameter to DynetParams to switch the behavior in case of fatal errors (i.e., throwing an exception or aborting).

issue_title: fatal error: mm_malloc.h: No such file or directory on ARM64
issue_body:
Hi dynet-users,

I tried to compile the latest `dynet` version on ARM64. I got the following error message:

```bash
 20%] Building CXX object dynet/CMakeFiles/dynet.dir/mem.cc.o
/media/ubuntu/speicher/repositories/dynet/dynet/mem.cc:13:24: fatal error: mm_malloc.h: No such file or directory
compilation terminated.
```

I could reproduce this error on two ARM64 systems:

* NVIDIA Jetson TX1 with Ubuntu 16.04 and GCC 5.4.0
* ODROID-C2 with Arch Linux and GCC 6.2.1

I guess it has something to do with the following macro defined in `mem.cc`:

```cpp
#if !_WINDOWS
	#include <mm_malloc.h>
#endif
```

As a temporary workaround I removed the `!_Windows` define and added these lines instead:

```cpp
#define _mm_free(p) free(p)
#define _mm_malloc(a, b) malloc(a)
```

inspired from [here](https://github.com/Microsoft/CNTK/blob/master/Source/Common/Include/ssefloat4.h). But I guess we need a more sophisticated solution here :)

issue_title: On renewing the CG when working with multiple modules
issue_body:
Hi,

When I use multiple modules within a single model, I notice that the training time increases significantly as the model is exposed to more training examples. I believe that there is something wrong in the way I am renewing the computational graph while combining the different modules but am unable to find it. Will appreciate help in debugging this. I am doing this:  For instance, if I have two different modules A and B such that A calls B, I am renewing the CG before calling A (for every example).  Here is an example module that uses 4 layer FFNNs as encoder and decoder networks in a VED ( Variational Encoder Decoder)

The loss function of the VED is as follows:
```
  def calculate_loss_basic(self, input, output):
       # Renew the computation graph
       #dy.renew_cg()  -> Not renewing the CG here but in the calling script that instantiates this.

       # Instantiate the params
       W_mean = dy.parameter(self.W_mean_p)
       V_mean = dy.parameter(self.V_mean_p)
       b_mean = dy.parameter(self.b_mean_p)
       W_var = dy.parameter(self.W_var_p)
       V_var = dy.parameter(self.V_var_p)
       b_var = dy.parameter(self.b_var_p)
       num_hidden = self.hidden_list[-1]
       
       enc_dnn = FeedForwardNeuralNet(self.model, [self.num_input, self.hidden_list, num_hidden, self.act])
       dec_dnn = FeedForwardNeuralNet(self.model, [num_hidden, self.hidden_list,self.num_output, self.act])

    
       # Get the output from encoder
       src_output = enc_dnn.predict(input)

       # Get the mean and log variance
       mu = self.mlp(src_output , W_mean, V_mean, b_mean)
       log_var = self.mlp(src_output , W_mean, V_mean, b_mean)       
    
       # Compute the KL Divergence loss
       kl_loss = -0.5 * dy.sum_elems( 1 + log_var - dy.pow(mu, dy.inputVector([2])) - dy.exp(log_var))

       # Reparameterize
       z = self.reparameterize(mu, log_var)
       
       return kl_loss, dec_dnn.calculate_loss(z , output)

```

The corresponding function in the FFNN is as follows:
```
  def predict(self, input):
    weight_matrix_array = []
    biases_array = []

    for (W,b, act) in zip(self.weight_matrix_array, self.biases_array, self.act):

    b = biases_array[0]

    activations = [intermediate]
    for (W,b, act) in zip(weight_matrix_array[1:], biases_array[1:], acts):
        pred =  act(W * activations[-1]  + b)
        activations.append(pred)
    return pred
```

The FFNN itself is declared as follows:

```

    self.W1 = self.pc.add_parameters((num_hidden_1, self.num_input))
    self.b1 = self.pc.add_parameters((num_hidden_1))
    
    # Add remaining layers
    self.weight_matrix_array = []
    self.biases_array = []
    self.weight_matrix_array.append(self.W1)
    self.biases_array.append(self.b1)
    for k in range(1, self.number_of_layers):
              if debug == 1: 
                   print "At ", k , " adding weights ", self.hidden_list[k], self.hidden_list[k-1]
              self.weight_matrix_array.append(self.model.add_parameters((self.hidden_list[k], self.hidden_list[k-1])))
              self.biases_array.append(self.model.add_parameters((self.hidden_list[k])))
    if debug == 1:
      print "Adding last hidden layer to output weights ", self.num_output, self.hidden_list[-1]
    self.weight_matrix_array.append(self.model.add_parameters((self.num_output, self.hidden_list[-1])))
    self.biases_array.append(self.model.add_parameters((self.num_output)))
    self.spec = (self.num_input, self.hidden_list, self.num_output, self.act)


```

The calling script is as follows:

```

       KL_loss, RECONS_loss = ved.calculate_loss_basic(dy.inputTensor(input_frame), dy.inputTensor(output_frame))

I've just run into a problem where we use environment variables in our include paths, like so:
```
include_directories(
  ${catkin_INCLUDE_DIRS}
  $ENV{dependency_ROOT}/include
  )
```

This leads to the following error:
```
error: build include path '$ENV{dependency_ROOT}/include' does not exist
```
Now, I'm aware the use of environment variables is strongly discouraged, but still, the path pointed to by this *does* exist.

I'm ready to put together a Pull Request that implements environment variable expansion, but I wanted to discuss if and where to make this change.

To me it seems like there are two obvious places to resolve environment variables: ```real_path()``` (in linter.py) and ```_resolve_vars()``` (in cmake.py).

I think changing ```_resolve_vars()``` (or adding an equivalent ```_resolve_env_vars()``` function makes the most sense.
Changing the behavior of ```_resolve_vars()``` would change the outcome of ```test_substitution()```, like so:
```
self.assertEqual(
    self.parse_all("cmd($ENV{PATH})"),
    [ ("cmd", [ "$ENV{PATH}" ]) ]
)
```
would become
```
self.assertEqual(
    self.parse_all("cmd($ENV{PATH})"),
    [ ("cmd", [ os.path.expandvars('${PATH}' ]) ]
)
```

One argument for changing ```real_path()``` instead is that linter.py already imports os and uses it heavily, whereas cmake.py does not.

What do you think?

issue_title: Remove pip install dependency on protoc
issue_body:
When the ONNX Python package becomes a dependency of a project, then currently the Protocol Buffers compiler becomes a dependency.

When installing the ONNX Python package many users get the following error:

```
Could not find "protoc" executable!
```

This is caused by the missing Protocol Buffers compiler. On some systems it's easy to install Protocol Buffers, but on Centos or Ubuntu 14.04 this is not so simple. Packaged versions of Protocol Buffers are older than 2.6.1, so users get these errors:

```
onnx.proto:308:7: Expected "required", "optional", or "repeated".
onnx.proto:308:19: Missing field number.
onnx.proto:330:3: Expected "required", "optional", or "repeated".
onnx.proto:330:15: Missing field number.
```

Would it be possible to remove the dependency on `protoc` from the `pip install` process?
Could the PyPI package be distributed with a precompiled form of the proto?

There is inconsistency in some of the operator signatures. Propose establish these guidelines and update any operators that do not follow them:
- Any variable represented by a single letter is capitalized (i.e. X)
- Any variable represented by a full word or multiple words is all lower case and uses underscores when it helps with readability
- Default type for integers will use variants on int, no uints, default is int64
- “axes” vs. “axis”
- “axes” is used when an input, output or attribute is representing multiple axis
- “axis” is used when an input, output or attribute is representing a single axis
- Any variable that represents a bias tensor will utilize the name “B”
- Any variable that represents a weight tensor will utilize the name “W”
- Any instance of multiplying a weight tensor by input tensor and adding bias we’ll following the format of W*X^T+B

issue_title: BatchNormalization sets wrong opset version be 1, not 6
issue_body:
ONNX checker reports error of BatchNormalization operator of consumed_input. However, this is opset version 1, but we emit opset version 6 of BatchNormalization

https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#BatchNormalization

> This version of the operator has been available since version 6 of the default ONNX operator set.
Other versions of this operator: BatchNormalization-1

our code is https://github.com/onnx/onnxmltools/blob/master/onnxmltools/convert/coreml/operator_converters/neural_network/BatchNorm.py#L69:
```python
    container.add_node(op_type, inputs, outputs, **attrs)
```
We use the default parameter `op_version=1`, here we should be 6.

issue_title: Allow 'file:' for tests_require and install_require in setup.cfg
issue_body:
Hi !

Most of the people use a `requirements.txt` file for managing their dependencies. Unfortunately, `setuptools` does not support reading from those files for the requirements tags, which means that devs have to either duplicate their requirements file in the `install_requires` parameter, or not use a requirement file at all. Since `setuptools` already supports reading some metadata from a file, it should probably be allowed to read those parameters as well.

issue_title: Overriding the variable seems doesn't work
downstream requests trigger an infinite loop in zipkin
issue_body:
Apply this diff:
```diff
diff --git a/tchannel/zipkin/zipkin_trace.py b/tchannel/zipkin/zipkin_trace.py
index 59d4db3..127e5e0 100644
--- a/tchannel/zipkin/zipkin_trace.py
+++ b/tchannel/zipkin/zipkin_trace.py
@@ -65,12 +65,14 @@ class ZipkinTraceHook(EventHook):
         return id < self._check_point

     def before_send_request(self, request):
+        print 'sending a req with', request.tracing.traceflags, request.tracing.trace_id, request.tracing.parent_span_id
         if not request.tracing.traceflags:
             return

         if not request.tracing.parent_span_id and not self._lucky(
             request.tracing.trace_id
         ):
+            print 'shedding load'
             # disable trace
             request.tracing.traceflags = False
             return
```

Instrument a simple server to trace 1% of requests:

```python
from tornado import gen
from tornado import ioloop

from tchannel import TChannel
from tchannel.zipkin.zipkin_trace import ZipkinTraceHook

tchannel = TChannel('my-cool-service')


@tchannel.json.register
@gen.coroutine
def endpoint(request):
    yield tchannel.json('some-other-service', 'foo')
    raise gen.Return({"hello": "world"})


@gen.coroutine
def main():
    yield tchannel.advertise(['10.29.39.18:21300'])
    zipkin_tracer = ZipkinTraceHook(tchannel, sample_rate=0.01)
    tchannel.hooks.register(zipkin_tracer)
    tchannel.listen()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
    ioloop.IOLoop.current().start()
```

Hit the endpoint *once* and you'll get an infinite stream of these messages:
```

issue_title: [Feature request]  To download remote files under specific virtual directory or container
issue_body:
Suppose I specify "CONTAINER_NAME/DIR1" for `--remote-path` param in download remote blob files, blobxfer dowlonads remote files with prefix matching like this:

```
---remote-path "CONTAINER_NAME/DIR1"
blobxfer does prefix matching with “CONTAINER_NAME/DIR1”, then give the following blobs as its result:
CONTAINER_NAME/DIR1/*   CONTAINER_NAME/DIR10/*   CONTAINER_NAME/DIR15/*, ….
```

My request is to be able to download remote files under specific virtual directory or container. For example, if I give "`mycontainer/foo`" to `--remote-path` param in downloading remote blobs files, I expect blobxfer to download either
-  blob files under `mycontainer/foo` virtual directory if foo is virtual directory
-  or blob file, `mycontainer/foo` if mycontainer/foo is an blob file

In order to support Profiles, the following create commands need to be converted to use either SDK calls or dynamic template generation.

- [ ] Application Gateway
- [ ] Express Route Circuit
- [ ] Load Balancer
- [ ] Local Gateway
- [ ] NIC
- [ ] NSG
- [ ] Public IP
- [ ] Traffic Manager Profile
- [ ] VNET

This is polishing like change regarding 4 existing commands under `vm access`, including `delete-linux-user`, `reset-linux-ssh`, `reset-windows-admin`, `set-linux-user`. The naming oddness reflects the service function difference between windows and linux vm, particularly on windows vm, you will not be able to add/rename/delete users, even though the API invoke always succeeds but nothing really happens at vm side
The change details are:
1. Rename category `vm access` to `vm user`
2. Collapse into 3 commands
    `delete`: delete a user from linux vm
    `update`: for windows vm, you can only update the admin password, for linux vm, you can add, or change both username and password, also you can upload public ssh key
    `reset-ssh`: reset linux vm’s SSH configurations

issue_title: az group export: query/output parameters not supported
issue_body:
1. The az group export command fails allways if the resource group has unknown schema types.

For Example: The schema of resource type 'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways' is not available.
If i try to export a Template I will allways get this message. But instead of giving me the template without the unknown schema i get nothing.  The Problem is: there a many unknown schemas 👎 

The Problem get even worese because the query parameter does not work either!

2. Query Parameter does not work. For example [?type=='Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts']" --> give me a Template for the storageAccounts. Does not work.

Since the azure portal is able to show me a deployment template the REST seems to be able deliver just a part of the resource group --> The issue must be the azure-cli.

---

### Environment summary

Install Method (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, edge build) / CLI version (`az --version`) / OS version / Shell Type (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  

```
```

The ~/.azure/accessTokens.json file that is generated as a result of az login (and read by e.g. the azurerm terraform module) contains refresh_token sections. To my understanding this means that when the access_tokens have expired, it should be possible to use the refresh_tokens to get new access tokens and avoid the need to actually log in again (which is a bit cumbersome for the manual login step that requires a browser visit).

My suggestion therefore is to add a flag "-refresh" to login and implement this.

CLI Version
2.0.31

OS Version
Windows 10 Pro, Version 1709

Python location 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft SDKs\Azure\CLI2\python.exe'

The following requirements are not validated by the ExpressRoute service, resulting in confusing errors and/or unexpected behavior:

1. Attempting to create MicrosoftPeering settings on an ExpressRoute circuit with a "Standard" SKU type yields a vague error ("An error occured.") and puts the circuit into a corrupt state. The service should validate that the SKU is compatible with the peering type and throw an actionable error.

2. Attempting to create MicrosoftPeering settings with a VLAN id that is already in use results in apparent success, but the peering relationship does not actually appear (for example, in the portal). The service should validate that the VLAN id is not in use or throw an actionable error.

issue_title: 'upload' should not remove common prefix when uploading folders
issue_body:
I'm trying to upload a tree of data using azure cli.
The folder structure looks something like "json/$project/$year/$month/$day/part_*.json"
I only have a couple of days data so, project,year,month are constant.

The command I issue is
az dls fs upload --source-path ./json --destination-path /json

My expectation was that the whole tree structure under json would be copied to DLS, as any other command like cp or rsync would do.

Instead, the $project/$year/$month part of the tree was omitted and I got a folder structure of  "json/$day/part_*.json"

It seems that the code computes a common prefix of all the files to be copied, and omits it.
For folder copying, this breaks expectations and should not be the case.

        async with session.get(RECORDS_URL) as response:
            resp = await response.json()
            print(resp)
            assert response.status == 200 or response.status == 403

async def delete_bucket(session):

def check_both_deleted_and_records(conn):
    cur = conn.cursor()
    cur.execute("SELECT * FROM records r JOIN deleted d ON (r.id = d.id AND r.parent_id = d.parent_id AND r.collection_id = d.collection_id);")

async def main():

        await asyncio.gather(*things)

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()

issue_body:

issue_title: "conda update package" does not update to latest version of package
issue_body:
If I build a package with the following minimal meta.yaml: 

``` yaml
package:
  name: some-package
  version: 1.0

build:
  number: 0
```

and do `conda install some-package` it gets installed correctly. Now i want to release a new version of my package with the additional dependency of twisted:

``` yaml
package:
  name: some-package
  version: 2.0

requirements:
  run:
    - twisted

build:
  number: 0
```

and do `conda update some-package` conda won't update some-package to the latest (2.0) version. If I do `conda update --all` it works fine and updates to version 2.0.

Is this a somehow intended behavior or a bug? Note that i did not specify a source section intentionally because i want to use this as a meta package. I also added my own channel and did not install it locally (what would imply --no-deps as I understand it).

This seems to be a regression in `conda` 4.0.

I have created two test packages just to check this. They are empty and have just the `meta.yaml`:

test-release:

``` yaml
package:
  name: test
  version: "1.0"

build:
  number: 0
  features:
```

test-debug:

``` yaml
package:
  name: test
  version: "1.0"

build:
  number: 1
  features:
    - debug

requirements:
  run:
    - debug
```

(`debug` is the anaconda metapackage that only tracks the `debug` feature.)

Now, when creating a new environment with only the `test` package, it tries to install the `debug` version:

``` sh
$ conda create -n test-pkg test=1.0
Fetching package metadata: ......
Solving package specifications: .........

Package plan for installation in environment D:\Miniconda\envs\test-pkg:

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    debug: 1.0-0
    test:  1.0-debug_1 [debug]

Proceed ([y]/n)?
```

issue_title: source activate borked due to PS1 prompt coloring.
issue_body:
TL;DR: my `.bash_profile` (included below) does `PS1` prompt coloring. Worked before, but now with Anaconda 4.3 `activate` borks part way through leaving me with a bad `PATH` and general brokenness.

I've checked as much as I can to confirm that both the `alias` operations and the `PS1` prompt coloring are working properly (including when I'm in a git repo and outside of one).  It all seems fine.  Until I use the Conda `activate` script.

Details (I only figured out the problem part way through writing this out):

Yesterday I did everything I could to annihilate every last piece of Anaconda on my laptop, removing directories, "dot" directories, "dot" files, etc. with abandon.  I then installed Anaconda 4.3 from scratch.  I've created one Conda environment called "rbokeh" that contains "r-essentials" and a bunch of other things.  I also clicked the "install R Studio" button in Navigator, so my root environment has had "r-essentials" (and other stuff) added to it.

Today when I tried to go back to my "rbokeh" environment I started up a new terminal session and invoked `source activate rbokeh`.  I got the following as output:

```
$ source activate rbokeh
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin/conda", line 6, in <module>
    sys.exit(conda.cli.main())
  File "/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 154, in main
    activate.main()
  File "/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/conda/cli/activate.py", line 135, in main
    activation_path = get_activate_path(shelldict)
  File "/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/conda/cli/activate.py", line 109, in get_activate_path
    binpath = binpath_from_arg(sys.argv[3], shelldict=shelldict)
  File "/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/conda/cli/activate.py", line 66, in binpath_from_arg
    prefix = prefix_from_arg(arg, shelldict=shelldict)
  File "/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/conda/cli/activate.py", line 58, in prefix_from_arg
    raise CondaValueError('Could not find environment: %s' % native_path)
conda.exceptions.CondaValueError: Value error: Could not find environment: /Users/ijstokes/anaconda/envs/dspyr
-bash: sed: command not found
-bash: grep: command not found
-bash: grep: command not found
-bash: sed: command not found
```

I have no idea where it could be picking up `/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/envs/dspyr`.  That was deleted along with everything else as part of my pre-Anaconda-4.3-annihilation.

But something bad happened as part of the `activate` script running, and I don't know what.

After getting this discouraging output I tried to see if I had a weird `activate` script on my `$PATH`, so I tried `which activate`.  This put me on to the fact that my `$PATH` was borked:

```
(rbokeh) ijstokes@0194-ijstokes DataSciencePythonR $ which activate
-bash: which: command not found
-bash: grep: command not found
-bash: sed: command not found

(rbokeh) ijstokes@0194-ijstokes DataSciencePythonR $ echo $PATH
/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/envs/rbokeh/bin:
-bash: grep: command not found
-bash: sed: command not found
```

I don't have time now to investigate much further, but perhaps others have seen this.

FWIW, I looked into my `~/.bash*` files to see if I had some weird settings in there, and I noticed that Anaconda is added 4 times to my `$PATH` (this is my complete `.bash_profile`:

```
# added by Anaconda 2.2.0 installer
export PATH="/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin:$PATH"

alias ls="ls -G"
alias less="less -r"
alias grep="grep --color=auto"

function color_my_prompt {
    local __user_and_host="\[\033[01;32m\]\u@\h"
    local __cur_location="\[\033[01;34m\]\W"
    local __git_branch_color="\[\033[31m\]"
    #local __git_branch="\`ruby -e \"print (%x{git branch 2> /dev/null}.grep(/^\*/).first || '').gsub(/^\* (.+)$/, '(\1) ')\"\`"
    local __git_branch='`git branch 2> /dev/null | grep -e ^* | sed -E  s/^\\\\\*\ \(.+\)$/\(\\\\\1\)\ /`'
    local __prompt_tail="\[\033[35m\]$"
    local __last_color="\[\033[00m\]"
    export PS1="$__user_and_host $__cur_location $__git_branch_color$__git_branch$__prompt_tail$__last_color "
}
color_my_prompt

export CONDA_ENVS=~/anaconda/envs

# added by Anaconda3 4.2.0 installer
export PATH="/Users/ijstokes/anconda3new/anaconda/bin:$PATH"

# added by Anaconda3 4.2.0 installer
export PATH="/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin:$PATH"

# added by Anaconda3 4.2.0 installer
export PATH="/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin:$PATH"

# added by Anaconda3 4.3.0 installer
export PATH="/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin:$PATH"
```

Fresh terminal session:

```
$ echo $PATH
/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin:/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin:/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin:/Users/ijstokes/anconda3new/anaconda/bin:/Users/ijstokes/anaconda/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin
```

Multiple `anaconda/bin` entries do not seem relevant: I've now removed them and the problem persists.

I wondered if it might be my `alias` operations, but no dice: commented these out and problem continuued.

issue_title: Matplotlib 1.5.3 doesn't work in Bash On Windows
issue_body:
With Anaconda2 v4.2.0, the packaged version of Matplotlib is 1.5.3-np111py27_0. It does not work when used in Bash On Windows because it is bundled with Qt 5 and for some reason it wants Qt 4. If you tried to just downgrade PyQt it still doesn't work. Solution I found was to downgrade to matplotlib 1.5.1.

```
sudo apt-get install libqtgui4
conda install matplotlib=1.5.1
```
Only with this and exporting DISPLAY to VcXsrv does matplotlib work. Is there any way to get 1.5.3 to work?

issue_title: conda clean -p should remove duplicate pacakge when there is multiple cache
issue_body:
We have an environment where we have a local installation of anaconda shared by many user on each computer. If user create an env and the shared installation do not contain the most up to date version of the packages, they will download new packages in there home. When we update later the shared conda installation, we end up with some packages duplicated in the home under "~/.conda/pkgs" and the in shared installation.

"conda clean -p" should update existing user env to point to the shared installation and delete from there home the duplicate packages.

This would remove much disk access to the home directory that is remote.

I don't know a good/easy enough manual way of doing this. Having this would help in the meantime.

I think the manual way to do this cleanly is:
- Find ourself which packages they have duplicate (very hard, each user create env at different time)
- ask user to uninstall the duplicate packages from all there env
- then do "conda clean -a"
- then reinstall the duplicate packages.

Do you think of a better way of doing this?
If tox.ini exist run all environments listed in `$ tox -l` as separate tasks.

I thought I'd look into compiling on my computer a standalone version of Pmag GUI using Pyinstaller which doesn't allow cross compiling, but as I have a windows distribution on half my hard drive this isn't an issue. Now 2 things came out of this:
- First occasionally Pmag GUI needs to know where some data files are so I couldn't just make a single binary I had to bundle those text files along with it and rewrite a little code to make sure it could always find it. Thanks to the good developing practices used for this import though it was relatively easy.
- Second Pyinstaller is not fast. It doesn't compile the python down to machine code in any way. As far as I can tell it just bundles a binary python interpreter in with binaries of the python files used and then on execution kind of on the run unpacks them and parses them through the bundled interpreter. It takes near a full 40 seconds to launch on my laptop.

So I guess the point of this issue is I would like to help continue support of a windows and maybe a linux binary as it seems a good way to help newcomers to the project and people who just want the program to run in their lab. Though this is a less than ideal binary as it has to be run next to the data models file

issue_title: Memory keeps increasing when user request data whose size < 1M (outbuf_overflow default )
issue_body:
My server program runs on windows(32bit) with Waitress + Pyramid.
When I tested an http API's performance with Apache ab, and  the results data's size < 1M, i found the the memory keep increasing  util Memory error thrown at last.

The ab command looks like below
```
 ab -c 10 -n 100000 -k http://apiurl
```
The issue only happen in keep alive mode and data size is smaller than 1M.
After debug, I found the cause:

- In keep alive mode, the http channel will not be closed in my test
- When data size < 1M, channel will use memory file BytesIO as output buffer
- In _flush_some function of channel.py, the output buffer will never be clear after all its data has been sent, see the code
```python
 while True:
            outbuf = self.outbufs[0]
            # use outbuf.__len__ rather than len(outbuf) FBO of not getting
            # OverflowError on Python 2
            outbuflen = outbuf.__len__()
            if outbuflen <= 0:
                # self.outbufs[-1] must always be a writable outbuf
                if len(self.outbufs) > 1:
                    toclose = self.outbufs.pop(0)
                    try:
                        toclose.close()
                    except:
                        self.logger.exception(
                            'Unexpected error when closing an outbuf')
                    continue # pragma: no cover (coverage bug, it is hit)
                else:
                    # issue here, when outbuflen < 0, outbufs[0] should be clear at least
                    dobreak = True
```

I added a line code in the marked place, then the issue could be workaround, please check if it is a reasonable fix:
```python
                else:
                    outbuf.prune()
                    dobreak = True
```

issue_title: Documentation of generated functions need to be clarified.
issue_body:
In mxnet, we use cython (or via ctypes on windows) to generate functions for NDArray and Symbol. However, the generated documentation is misleading.

Take `mxnet.symbol.mean` as an example, [the documentation](http://mxnet.io/api/python/symbol.html#mxnet.symbol.mean) says:

~~~
mxnet.symbol.mean(*args, **kwargs):

    Parameters:	
        data (NDArray) – Source input
        axis (Shape(tuple), optional, default=()) – Empty or unsigned or tuple. The axes
            to perform the reduction.If left empty, a global reduction will be performed.
        keepdims (boolean, optional, default=False) – If true, the axis which is reduced
            is left in the result as dimension with size one.
        name (string, optional.) – Name of the resulting symbol.
    Returns:	
        symbol – The result symbol.
    Return type:	
        Symbol
~~~

Notice that the parameter `data`'s type is `NDArray`. However, when we pass a ndarray as argument to this function, **an error will be raised**:

~~~python
In [1]: a = ndarray.array([[1,2], [3,4]])

In [2]: type(a)
Out[2]: mxnet.ndarray.NDArray

In [3]: symbol.mean(a)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypeError                                 Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-7-37f4ed75ee14> in <module>()
----> 1 symbol.mean(a)

Anaconda\lib\site-packages\mxnet-0.9.3-py3.5.egg\mxnet\_ctypes\symbol.py in creator(*args, **kwargs)
    189         hint = func_name.lower()
    190         name = NameManager.current.get(name, hint)
--> 191         s._compose(*args, name=name, **symbol_kwargs)
    192         return s
    193

Anaconda\lib\site-packages\mxnet-0.9.3-py3.5.egg\mxnet\symbol.py in _compose(self, *args, **kwargs)
    235         for arg in args:
    236             if not isinstance(arg, Symbol):
--> 237                 raise TypeError('Compose expect `Symbol` as arguments')
    238         for val in kwargs.values():
    239             if not isinstance(val, Symbol):

TypeError: Compose expect `Symbol` as arguments
~~~

Notice that when apply `mxnet.symbol.mean` to a ndarray, a `TypeError` is raised. According to the documentation, however, the program is valid.

The root of this issue is that we have registered all functions from `MXListAllOpNames` both into `mxnet.ndarray` and `mxnet.symbol` modules when these two modules are imported, regardless what there argument types actual are!

A possible solution for this trouble is do some analysis in function `_make_ndarray_function` and `_make_atomic_symbol_function`, rather than register all operators and provide misleading documentation.

issue_title: Cannot call inspect.getinnerframes on Traceback instance
issue_body:
`ExceptionInfo` creates a `Traceback` instance from the traceback provided by `sys.exc_info` and then throws the traceback away. This is inconvenient because some tools (in my case, https://github.com/MindscapeHQ/raygun4py/ calls `inspect.getinnerframes` on the provided traceback) want to inspect the traceback of an error to get info for error reporting, but the `Traceback` instance cannot be inspected by functions like `inspect.getinnerframes`.

Either a reference to the original traceback object or somehow subclassing the built-in class would make it a lot easier to plug these errors into error reporting libraries that expect native traceback objects.

issue_title: TypeScript: Would be useful to have named types for complex args
issue_body:
Seems like this might already be tracked somewhere, but I couldn't find an issue. Apologies if I missed something.

The TypeScript declarations are super helpful for discovering what options are available on various resources. However, there are many deeply nested configuration objects that are built almost entirely of `pulumi.Input`, anonymous objects, and primitives. If you make a small error in a nested attribute, `tsc` produces pretty large and obnoxious error messages that are difficult to read. The entire root configuration object gets a squiggly red underline!

I've found that a great way to deal with this problem is to build up larger objects incrementally and assign explicit types to the intermediate values. Although restructuring syntax helps, this is a little more verbose, but well worth it, as it significantly narrows the scope of compiler errors.

Sometimes, you can use fancy TypeScript features like indexed types to get at a useful anonymous type for this purpose. For example:

```typescript
let defaultCacheBehavior: aws.cloudfront.DistributionArgs['defaultCacheBehavior'] = { ...
```

But that's pretty kludgy and sometimes not possible, for example when something is wrapped in `pulumi.Input<...>[]`.

Would be nice if all of these various properties had usefully named types. For this example, I'd love to write:

```typescript
let defaultCacheBehavior: aws.cloudfront.DefaultCacheBehavior = { ...
```

Generally, there are sensible names for these things in the CloudFormation documentation. Again, for example: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-cloudfront-distribution-defaultcachebehavior.html

issue_title: 100% CPU usage with pyzmq socket when sending huge data.
issue_body:
I am currently in the process of implementing a communication pipeline between several processes using pyzmq, all using the Push/Pull mechanism. I write a Pub/Sub pipeline to generate/receive tasks and a Push/Pull pipeline to send/get data and here is where my problem also starts: ZeroMQ seems to be using 100% CPU load when I am trying to send a huge xml data(size: 150MB, type: string) using ```socket.send()```.

Here is the code in question, which attempts to send just one message:

``` python
# netengine.py
class NetEngine:

        # 初始化网络通道
        self.context = zmq.Context()
        self.sender = self.context.socket(zmq.PUSH)
        self.receiver = self.context.socket(zmq.SUB)
        self.parser_ip = _parser_ip
        self.push_port = _push_port
        self.sub_port = _sub_port
        self.messager = Message() # message analysiser

    def net_init(self):
        # 网络通道端口绑定
        self.sender.connect("tcp://%s:%s" % (self.parser_ip, self.push_port))
        self.receiver.connect("tcp://%s:%s" % (self.parser_ip, self.sub_port))

    def send_msg(self, str):
        self.sender.send(str)

    def on_recv(self, cmd):
        front_back_print("incomming event:{}".format(cmd))

    def net_loop(self):
        """
        开启网络循环,与分析端对接,登录并交换设备信息
        :return: void
        """
        front_back_print('Main runs as %s' % (os.getpid()))
        self.receiver.setsockopt(zmq.SUBSCRIBE, "UID_{}".format(uid))  # 订阅分析端发布消息的uid通道
        front_back_print('---- Sending login information ----')
        login_info = {'uid': uid, 'group_id': group_id, 'my_ip': host,
                      'role': device_role, 'type': device_type}  # 初始化login报文信息
        self.send_msg("{}".format(self.messager.buildMsg('login', login_info)))  # 发送login报文

        while True:
            """
            持续从分析端接收报文
            完成login握手
            """
            msg = self.receiver.recv().split(' ', 1)[1]
            cmd, msg_info = self.messager.parseMsg(msg)

            # 网络引擎处理login操作,其余报文交给event_exec()处理
            if cmd == 'login':
                # 分析端信息, 目前并不不会用到以下信息, 先注释掉了
                # _parser_uid = msg_info['uid']
                # _parser_group_id = msg_info['group_id']
                _parser_ip = msg_info['my_ip']
                _parser_device_role = msg_info['role']
                _parser_device_type = msg_info['type']

                front_back_print('Parser ip: ' + _parser_ip + ' & device_role: ' + _parser_device_role +
                                 ' & divice_type: ' + _parser_device_type)
                front_back_print('Login successfully!')
            else:
                self.on_recv(cmd)
                ee.event_exec(cmd, msg_info)  # 传给base插件进行业务处理

            time.sleep(1)

    def net_close(self):
        """
        关闭网络引擎接口
        :return: void
        """
        self.receiver.close()
        self.sender.close()
        self.context.term()

```

This is code of my net engine.

And when I run code as follow:

issue_title: Inconsistent passing of **kwargs to CustomRecurrentLayer's child layers
issue_body:
`CustomRecurrentLayer` doesn't pass `kwargs` to `hidden_to_hidden` when calling `get_output`:
https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/layers/recurrent.py#L387
It does for in_to_hid when `precompute_input` is `True`:
https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/layers/recurrent.py#L370
but not when `precompute_input` is `False`:
https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/layers/recurrent.py#L394

Intuitively, I'd think it should always pass `**kwargs` to its child layers, but I have a vague feeling there may be a case when the user wouldn't want it to.  Is there?  If not, I will fix this and make it pass them.

issue_title: Make sure that module and define states are reset when doing a configuration rollback
issue_body:
Apache plugin maintains a state of active `LoadModule` and `Define` statements in the configuration. When doing a configuration rollback, the state is currently not automatically reset. 

This potentially leads to Apache plugin state being out-of-sync with the actual configuration present in the system.

The task requires restructuring of `AugeasConfigurator` <-> `ApacheConfigurator` <-> `ApacheParser` relations, as rollback functionality currently resides in `AugeasConfigurator` and the afformentioned states are completely handled in `ApacheParser`

Steps to reproduce:
1. Set up certbot development environment.
2. Make sure neither Nginx nor Apache is installed.
3. Run `certbot --config-dir /tmp --logs-dir /tmp --work-dir /tmp --staging`

Expected result: Error about no installer plugins (#3504).

Actual result: Error about installer plugins is printed in text at the bottom of the screen. A different error message is left in the dialog window above it. See screenshot.

![two-errors](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/220205/18605098/85f26da0-7c3d-11e6-8870-144ea5b252fc.png)

If you run `certbot-auto --os-packages-only`, `getopts` parses the `n`, thinks you mean `-n` and forces yes in your package manager.

To do what we want in a portable way, I think we'll have to do argument parsing by hand.
